title: cluster_by_scribal_provenance data payload
description: v0.58 two-tier scribal provenance clustering — port of wallet-fingerprint v0.7's funded_by / first_out_to separation back to cuneiform. Buckets tablets by shared first_copy_event (earliest manuscript witness, the 'scribal find-spot of the text') and/or shared first_citation_target (first commentary citing the tablet, the 'purpose-of-creation' signal). Optionally classifies a specific pair via tablet_id_a + tablet_id_b. Complements v0.48's find_provenance_clusters, which buckets by ancient geographic find-spot — this tool buckets by textual lineage event.

clusters_first_copy.description: Tablets bucketed by shared first_copy_event (earliest manuscript witness). Singletons dropped via min_members. Sorted by member-count desc.

members.description: Tablet IDs whose earliest manuscript witness is `key`.

clusters_first_citation.description: Tablets bucketed by shared first_citation_target (first commentary citing them). Singletons dropped via min_members. Sorted by member-count desc.

key.description: The shared first_citation_target commentary tablet ID.

members.description: Tablet IDs whose first citation lands on `key`.

relationship.description: Wallet-fingerprint v0.7 enum: shared-copy-event ≈ shared-funder; shared-citation-target ≈ shared-first-out; shared-both = strongest signal; different = both sides known but disjoint; unknown = at least one side missing data.

pair_classification.description: Null unless both tablet_id_a and tablet_id_b were provided. When provided, classifies the pair's provenance relationship.

tablets_examined.description: Total tablets in the per-tablet provenance index.

witnesses_present.description: Tablets whose first_copy_event resolved to a non-null ID.

citations_present.description: Tablets whose first_citation_target resolved to a non-null ID.

metadata_coverage_pct.description: Percent of examined tablets with at least one provenance signal (witness OR citation). Low coverage → low cluster yield; surface a warning.
